mappings.rationale: Directly covers the art of music, musical thinking, performance, production, and consumption -- the core subject of this entire section (A-G).
Examines orchestral performance, instrumentation, and orchestration from Haydn's classical ensembles to the modern symphony orchestra, directly relevant to sections B.3, C.7, and E.1.a.
Traces medieval plainchant, Renaissance polyphony, and Baroque forms including liturgical music, relevant to Western music history and vocal/choral genres (E.1.b, G.1).
Beethoven is central to the sonata, symphony, and concerto forms explicitly listed in section E.2.a, and to Western music history (G.1).
Traces the blues from Mississippi Delta origins through urban electrification, exploring its foundational role in jazz development and popular music (E.2.b, E.4).
Directly covers folk music as a genre determined by cultural milieu, including its relation to popular and art music (E.4).
Traces country music from Appalachian roots through the Nashville sound, illustrating a popular genre shaped by cultural milieu (E.4).
Surveys music across global cultures, directly relevant to non-Western musical traditions and genres determined by cultural milieu (E.4).
Provides the academic framework for studying music across cultures, relevant to writings about music and non-Western traditions (A.5, E.4).
Investigates musical perception, emotional response, and performance psychology, directly relevant to musical meaning and human activities (A.2, A.4).
Addresses electrophonic instruments, electronic music, and recording technology from the phonograph to digital audio, directly relevant to sections B.3.d, E.1.c, and F.
Focuses on incidental and background music for motion pictures, explicitly listed under music for the theatre (E.3.c.iv).
Explains the physics of tone, pitch, frequency, and resonance, directly relevant to the physical aspects of musical sound (B.1, C).
Adorno's philosophy of music and cultural theory are directly relevant to writings about music and problems of musical meaning (A.2, A.5).
